meta_title: For Freelancers: Git with AI to Solve no understanding of APIs (Intermediate Tutorial)
meta_description: Learn how to Git with AI and overcome no understanding of APIs. A Intermediate-level Tutorial using Antigravity and Claude Code.
h2_structure: The Problem: no understanding of APIs

content.seo_title: Git with AI: Overcome API Hurdles for Freelancers
content.seo_description: Stop struggling with APIs. Learn how to leverage Git with AI using Antigravity and Claude Code to ship complex integrations at record speed.
content.answer_block: You don't need to be an API expert to build robust integrations. By using Claude Code to analyze documentation and Antigravity to manage Git workflows, you can automate boilerplate, debug request cycles, and ship production-ready API connectors without writing manual network-layer code. Focus on the logic; let AI handle the syntax.
content.intro:
The clock is ticking on your client’s deadline, and you’re staring at a wall of cryptic JSON responses and 403 Forbidden errors. You aren't an API architect, and frankly, you don't have time to become one. The gap between your creative vision and the technical execution is widening, and manual debugging is eating your margins.

But what if your Git repository became an intelligent partner rather than just a storage unit? By integrating AI directly into your version control and development lifecycle, you transform static documentation into live, executable code. This isn't just about writing faster; it's about shifting the burden of technical complexity to tools that were built to master it.

steps.title: Contextualize the API Documentation
steps.description: Before writing code, feed the API's OpenAPI or Swagger specs to your AI agent to build a mental map of endpoints and authentication requirements.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Analyze the provided API documentation. Create a summary of the authentication flow, base URL, and the three most critical endpoints for this project.

steps.title: Automate Boilerplate Generation
steps.description: Avoid manual HTTP request setup. Use AI to generate robust, typed client wrappers based on the documentation you just analyzed.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Generate a clean, modular TypeScript client for the API endpoints identified. Include error handling for common status codes.
steps.claude_code_command: claude write 'Create a client class in src/api/client.ts using the spec provided'

steps.title: Version Control with AI Context
steps.description: Never write a vague commit message again. Let AI analyze your changes and generate semantic commits that reflect the API integration logic.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Analyze the current diff in the staging area and write a conventional commit message following the 'feat: API integration' format.
steps.claude_code_command: claude git commit -m 'feat: implement oauth2 flow for external service'

steps.title: Debug API Failures via Logs
steps.description: Instead of guessing why a request failed, pipe your error logs directly into your AI agent to get an instant diagnosis and fix.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Analyze these error logs from the API response. Identify the missing headers or payload mismatch and provide the corrected code snippet.

steps.title: Implement Automated Regression Tests
steps.description: Ensure your API integration doesn't break when you update your code by generating unit tests for your client wrappers.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Create unit tests for the API client using Vitest, mocking the network requests to ensure stability during CI/CD.
steps.claude_code_command: claude write 'Generate tests/api.test.ts for the client in src/api/client.ts'

pro_tips: Always store your API specs in a /docs folder in your repo so the AI can index them as primary context.
Use 'claude code' to run local linting fixes automatically before pushing to your main branch.
Create a custom .claudeprompt file in your root to define your specific coding standards and preferred libraries.
When debugging, provide the AI with the exact request/response payload, not just the error message.
Use Antigravity to manage feature branches specifically for API integrations to keep your main branch stable during testing.
content.next_steps: Begin by initializing a new repository, adding your documentation, and running your first Claude Code session to generate a functional API client.
content.conversion_block: Stop fighting documentation and start shipping high-value features for your clients. Leverage the power of AI-assisted Git workflows to scale your freelance business today. Ready to build? Join our AI Builder community → Start your first project today.
